role: Registered Client Service Assistant/ 401(k) Client Service Assistant (Richmond, Suffolk or Virginia Beach)

matched_keywords.context: Summary states "audit and compliance professional" and Skills include "Internal Audit Support"; experience describes audit-style reviews and audit planning support.
Summary mentions "consumer compliance adherence"; Skills include "Consumer Compliance Monitoring"; experience assesses compliance with policies/procedures.
Key Skills explicitly lists "Operational Audits"; work describes operational process evaluations and adherence reviews.
Summary notes strengthening internal controls; Skills include "Internal Controls Testing"; experience references control points and control improvements.
Summary includes "control testing" and Skills include "Internal Controls Testing".
Summary includes "walkthrough-style reviews"; Skills include "Walkthroughs & Process Understanding"; experience includes "walkthrough-style evaluations".
Summary includes "audit scoping"; Skills include "Audit Scope & Planning Support"; experience includes defining review scope.
Summary includes "workpaper-quality documentation"; Skills include "Workpaper Documentation"; experience documents results/exceptions with evidence.
Summary mentions reporting actionable findings; experience includes preparing written reports and process narratives summarizing findings.
Skills include "Risk/Control Identification"; experience references identifying high-risk themes and control points.
Skills include "Data Analysis"; experience includes building dashboards to analyze trends and error rates.
Summary and Skills list Power BI; experience includes building and maintaining Power BI dashboards.
Summary and Skills list advanced Excel; experience includes Excel dashboards and reconciliation.
Skills include cross-functional collaboration; experience describes partnering with compliance and business leaders.
Work experience is at CarMax (Quality Assurance Consultant).

suggested_additions.reason: Job heavily emphasizes SOX 404 ICFR testing; candidate already highlights internal controls testing and walkthroughs, so clarifying any SOX exposure (even indirect/supporting) would improve alignment if true.

Role is focused on ICFR; candidate discusses internal controls broadly—adding ICFR terminology where applicable would better match ATS searches if their controls work touched reporting/metrics governance.
JD references reporting for management and Audit Committee; candidate already prepares leadership reports—adding explicit phrasing about executive-ready reporting would be plausible.
Audits commonly require sampling; candidate mentions checklists and scope and appears to do review-based testing—adding "sampling" (if used) is plausible and ATS-relevant.
JD expects recommendations and improvements; candidate mentions corrective action follow-up—adding explicit remediation tracking language would strengthen fit.
Candidate writes process narratives and performs walkthroughs; process mapping is a natural adjacent skill often used in audit documentation.
JD specifically mentions CFPB oversight; candidate has consumer compliance monitoring—if their work aligned to CFPB-related requirements, naming it would improve match.
JD mentions integrated audits with IT audit; candidate references multiple systems and dashboards—if they partnered with IT/security/data teams, adding that collaboration is plausible.
Tableau is a plus; candidate already uses Power BI and advanced Excel, making Tableau familiarity plausible if they have had exposure.
JD stresses managing multiple projects; candidate lists time management—adding explicit multi-project delivery/deadlines language (if accurate) would help.

fabrication_warnings.reason: Resume frames experience as "audit and compliance" with "audit-style reviews" in a QA consultant role; may be interpreted as internal audit experience, but it is not clearly financial/internal audit or public accounting auditing as required by the JD.

ats_result.summary: Resume aligns well with consumer compliance, operational review work, walkthroughs, control testing concepts, and strong documentation/reporting—key components of internal audit execution. The largest gaps versus the role are explicit SOX 404/ICFR experience, public accounting audit background, and financial reporting/SEC filing exposure (10-Q/10-K). Adding precise audit terminology (sampling, remediation tracking, ICFR) and any verified SOX/financial-controls involvement would materially improve ATS alignment.
